mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and discount rates for future cash flows, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in low-rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.5 percent, consumer spending power collapses and corporate earnings forecasts are downgraded, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in greater than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 8.5 percent for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts signal synchronized consumer and business demand expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative forward returns in greater than 60 percent of occurrences over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production reflects immediate capacity utilization gains and supply-side momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in greater than 50 percent of months where INDPRO rises, specifically during late-cycle expansions.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 218.0, anticipated monetary tightening compresses equity valuations and increases discount rates, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 50 percent of months where CPIAUCSL surpasses 218.0.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a decline in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative forward returns in greater than 55 percent of observed months over a 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth directly correlates with improved corporate revenue expectations and earnings revisions, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days following data release.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no statistical significance against GDPC1 growth rates over a 3-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index falls below 95.0, manufacturing contraction signals weakening economic activity and reduced capital expenditure, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in greater than 50 percent of months where INDPRO drops below 95.0.
